agent_idea: Eliminate the `scale_applied` boolean and its per-layer branch from `second_half`'s scaled path by handling the first (scale-folded) layer before the loop and looping over the remaining layers directly, removing unnecessary branch instructions from the inverse DFT hot path.

Add `#[inline]` hints to the five key layer functions (`dit_layer`, `dit_layer_rev`, `dit_layer_rev_scaled`, `dit_layer_oop`, `dit_layer_twiddle_free`, `dit_layer_first_one`) to allow LLVM to inline them into Rayon parallel closures, enabling better cross-boundary optimization like eliminating the `backwards` branch and improving register allocation in the butterfly hot loops.

Re-apply the iter #001 restructuring of `second_half` to eliminate the per-layer `scale_applied` boolean branch (giving ~+0.48% previously), combined with simplifying `dit_layer_rev_scaled` to take `scale: F` directly instead of `Option<F>`, removing the dead `None` arm and reducing overall code size.

Pre-broadcast `scale` into a packed field (`F::Packing::from(scale)`) once outside the per-block loop in `dit_layer_rev_scaled`'s `Some(s)` branch, via a new `apply_to_rows_with_packed_scale` method on `ScaledDitButterfly`, avoiding a redundant broadcast per block that the compiler might not automatically hoist from the inlined call.

Manually unroll the layer loop in `second_half_general` by 2 to pass `false` and `true` as literal constants to `dit_layer_rev`, allowing LLVM (via `#[inline]`) to specialize each call and DCE the dead iteration-order branch from the hot butterfly loop.

agent_idea: Apply backwards-constant-unrolling only to `first_half_general_oop` (not `first_half_general`), passing literal `true`/`false` at each `dit_layer` call site to allow LLVM to DCE the dead block-iteration-order branch, isolating the benefit from `first_half_general_oop` which may be the primary source of improvement in previous +1.06%/+0.68% weak-signal results since `first_half_general` alone regressed (-0.18%).

Remove the alternating `backwards` flag from `first_half_general` and `first_half_general_oop` (always pass `false` to `dit_layer`), eliminating the runtime `if backwards { blocks.rev() } else { blocks }` branch from these hot-path functions — since with `#[inline]` on `dit_layer`, LLVM was already receiving the alternating bool as a runtime value (not a constant), so it could not DCE the dead branch; now with a literal constant, the false branch is completely eliminated from the generated code.

Extend iter #015's "always pass `false`" approach (which got +0.75% weak signal from `first_half_general` + `first_half_general_oop`) by also removing the alternating `backwards` flag from `second_half_general`, eliminating the runtime `if backwards { blocks.rev() }` branch from all three coset-DFT coordination functions simultaneously so LLVM can DCE the dead branch from each inlined `dit_layer`/`dit_layer_rev` specialization.

Re-apply iter #015's "always pass `false` to `dit_layer`" optimization in `first_half_general` and `first_half_general_oop`, eliminating the alternating `backwards` runtime branch from these two coset DFT coordination functions — this previously showed a +0.75% weak signal that just missed the p<0.05 threshold, and is the strongest repeatable signal seen across the entire run.

Add an `apply_to_rows` override to `TwiddleFreeButterfly` that explicitly computes `sum = x1+x2` and `diff = x1-x2` before assigning them back to `*x1` and `*x2`, avoiding the default path's `apply_in_place` → tuple-assign indirection, potentially allowing LLVM to generate cleaner register allocation for the add/sub operations used in `dit_layer_twiddle_free` (layer 0 of `first_half`) and `dit_layer_first_one` (first row-pair of layers 1..mid-1 of `first_half`).

agent_idea: Remove the alternating `backwards` flag from `second_half_general` by always passing `false` to `dit_layer_rev`, eliminating the per-iteration `backwards = !backwards` flip and the runtime branch in the coordination loop, reducing overhead in the coset DFT second half.

Remove the alternating `backwards` flag from `first_half` (the non-general inverse DFT first half), always passing `false` to `dit_layer_twiddle_free` and `dit_layer_first_one`, eliminating the runtime backwards branch from the hot inverse DFT path (which processes the full 2^20×256 matrix in `coset_lde_batch`) by allowing LLVM to DCE the dead `if backwards { blocks.rev() }` branch from both inlined functions.

Combine removal of the alternating `backwards` flag from `first_half` (always passing `false`, eliminating a per-layer branch/flip) with elimination of the `scale_applied` bool from `second_half`'s scaled path (handling the first scaled layer before the loop, removing a per-iteration branch), targeting two independent hot-path functions in `coset_lde_batch` that previously each showed ~+0.4-0.73% weak signal individually.

Combine `TwiddleFreeButterfly::apply_to_rows` override (avoids tuple temporary from default `apply_in_place` path) with always-passing `false` to `backwards` in `first_half` (eliminating the per-layer runtime backwards-branch from the hot `dit_layer_twiddle_free` and `dit_layer_first_one` inlined calls), targeting two orthogonal code paths within the same hot `first_half` function for an expected ~1.1% combined improvement.
